Core Concepts in Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications

Synthesis note on recurring concepts in Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications.

Source

This synthesis draws across the chapter notes for Evaluation Theory, Models, and Applications (Stufflebeam and Coryn 2014).

Central Logic

The book treats evaluation as a standards-guided practice for producing defensible judgments of value and helping users improve programs, make decisions, and meet accountability obligations.

Concept Map

Concepts

Value and Judgment

  • merit and worth: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • probity: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • value criteria: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • justified conclusions: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • consumer needs: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.

Standards and Accountability

  • utility: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • feasibility: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • propriety: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • accuracy: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • evaluation accountability: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • metaevaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.

Approach Families

  • pseudoevaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • quasi-evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • improvement and accountability: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • social agenda and advocacy: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • eclectic evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.

Selected Models

  • CIPP: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • consumer-oriented evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • responsive evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • utilization-focused evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • case study evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • experimental design evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.

Evaluation Process

  • opportunity assessment: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • evaluation design: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • budgeting: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • contracting: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • information collection: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • analysis and synthesis: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
  • communication and use: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence, interpretation, quality, or use.
